name: Stream-Oriented Programming

corePrinciples: Time, windowing and backpressure are first-class
x.controlDataFlow: Data flows through a graph of operators (map/filter/window/join); processing is push or pull with backpressure.
x.suitableProjects: Real-time analytics, event processing, log/metric pipelines, and continuous ETL.

def: A style that models computation over unbounded, possibly real-time sequences of data, using lazy operators with windowing and backpressure.
problem: Batch thinking does not fit data that arrives continuously and must be processed with bounded memory and latency.
mechanism: Builds a dataflow graph of stream operators; events flow through transformations and time/count windows, with backpressure to bound buffering. Stateful operators maintain aggregates across the stream.
failures: Unbounded state growth in keyed aggregations
whenNot: Tiny data that fits a simple loop
myths: "Streaming is just fast batch" — windowing, watermarks and ordering make it a distinct model
